title: callicarpa subpubescens
intro: Callicarpa subpubescens, also known as the purple fruit callicarpa, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to Southeast Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant's health benefits include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. Callicarpa subpubescens is often prepared as a tea or used in herbal infusions, and can also be made into a tincture or decoction for medicinal purposes.
